name: ratified decisions (ADR index)
description: Auth is self-hosted Better Auth (magic-link) on our own DB. Open ADR-0005 before changing the auth mechanism.

name: emailOTP alongside magic-link via AuthMailPort
description: Better Auth emailOTP (better-auth/plugins, otpLength 6) coexists with magic-link through one AuthMailPort (superset of core MailPort: sendMagicLink + sendLoginCode). OTP is a separate email (not merged); codes reuse the existing verification table (no migration); requestLoginCode(email) is the Zod-validated server seam over auth.api.sendVerificationOTP.

A module-level fake singleton in @resonance/auth is NOT shared across Next.js dev route-handler scopes — the Better Auth catch-all (writer) and a separate test route (reader) got different devFake buffers, so peekLoginCode returned null.
resolution: Pin the fake transport to a globalThis singleton so all Next.js route-handler module scopes share one buffer.

name: Live Resend transport + per-seam key precedence
description: createResendMail(apiKey, from) sends magic-link + OTP via the Resend SDK behind the existing AuthMailPort (no interface change); errors thrown not swallowed. resolveMail precedence: RESEND_API_KEY -> live Resend (wins over RESONANCE_FAKES so live email works with fake AI), else fake, else fail-closed stub. Consequence: keep RESEND_API_KEY out of the fakes-E2E env or peekLoginCode finds no fake code. Default sender onboarding@resend.dev only delivers to the Resend account owner until a domain is verified (RESEND_FROM_EMAIL).

title: resolveMail is live-by-default (ADR-0018) — no RESONANCE_FAKES branch; fake is test-injected via @resonance/auth/testing
rationale: ADR-0018 forbids env-flag fake selection in shipped runtime code. resolveMail() now returns live Resend when RESEND_API_KEY is set, else a fail-closed stub that throws on send (explicit degrade). createFakeMail moved out of the runtime module (mail.ts) to a test-only subpath export @resonance/auth/testing (mirrors @resonance/db/testing), injected via createAuth({ mail }). auth-secret dev fallback is now gated on NODE_ENV=test only (RESONANCE_FAKES disjunct removed); prod fails closed. Supersedes the fake tier in the old per-seam precedence (mx-23d37c).

name: peekLoginCode observes a DI-injected fake via a process-wide slot (no env gate)
description: To keep peekLoginCode working after removing RESONANCE_FAKES: the test-only createFakeMail registers its captured codes array into a globalThis observation slot on construction; peekLoginCode (kept on the main @resonance/auth entrypoint because apps/web /api/test/last-otp imports it) reads that slot with no env gate. Inert in production since nothing constructs a fake there. The global slot preserves cross-Next.js-module-scope observation (the reason the fake was globalThis-pinned, mx-b19c21). Final disposition of peekLoginCode + /api/test/last-otp + the deterministic OTP E2E is handed to seed resonance-a4a4 (separate, human-gated).

name: otp-readback-explicit-observe
description: peekLoginCode's cross-scope OTP read-back is fed ONLY by an explicit observeLoginCodes(fake) opt-in on @resonance/auth/testing; createFakeMail no longer registers on construction (kills action-at-a-distance / last-writer-wins hijack). peekLoginCode is doubly inert in prod: nothing registers there AND a NODE_ENV==='production' guard short-circuits the read. The apps/web E2E harness pins its fake to globalThis and calls observeLoginCodes exactly once, so session reads routed through getWebAuth can't clobber the read-back.

title: apps/web session reads go through getWebAuth (getWebSession) — one Better Auth instance per process
rationale: getSession(headers, auth?) is now generic over the Better Auth instance (defaults to getAuth()); apps/web adds getWebSession(headers)=getSession(headers, await getWebAuth()) and all shell callers (actions.ts, page.tsx) use it, so the mount and session reads share ONE instance instead of two that merely agreed via shared BETTER_AUTH_SECRET+DATABASE_URL. Harness selection stays in the shell (ADR-0018 §4) — no fakes flag pushed into @resonance/auth. The harness auth instance + fake mail are globalThis-pinned so 'one per process' holds across Next.js route/RSC module scopes (mulch mx-b19c21), which also prevents a session read from clobbering the OTP read-back.
